title: rubus emancipatus
side_effects: Rubus emancipatus can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps, due to its high levels of tannins and anthocyanins. Prolonged use or excessive consumption may also lead to kidney damage and an increased risk of bleeding disorders. Additionally, individuals with allergies or sensitivities may experience skin irritation, hives, or anaphylaxis upon contact with the plant's sap or leaves. In severe cases, improper use of Rubus emancipatus can cause liver toxicity and other systemic complications.
